entry.title: How to Prepare for the SAP BTP Solution Architect Certification (with Sample Questions)
entry.content:
In this blog, I’ll share practical tips to help you to prepare and be able to pass the SAP BTP Solution Architect certification (P_BTPA_2408). While the post is especially helpful for those aiming to get certified, it also provides useful insights for SAP developers, architects, project managers, and anyone daily involved in SAP BTP projects or operations.

Being a successful SAP Solution Architect or Enterprise Architect requires a deep understanding of both on-premises and hyperscaler technologies, combined with hands-on experience from real customer projects. On top of that, strong expertise in SAP solutions is essential. Earning the SAP Solution Architect certification is just the beginning of this journey.

In these blog series, I’ll share practical insights to help you prepare and succeed to achieve this goal.

🎓 Why Get SAP Certified? Real-World Benefits:

  • Being certified earns immediate credibility customers, partners, and hiring managers are more likely to trust your knowledge and decisions.
  • SAP certification improves your chances of landing your desired role.
  • Certified professionals often qualify for higher rates, promotions, and leadership roles in SAP projects.
  • In some cases (e.g., security reviews, migration, SOC documentation), certification adds weight to your approval or sign-off.
  • The annual certification renewal process ensures your knowledge stays current, especially with cloud solutions and SAP's evolving product roadmap.
  • Certification validates your hands-on skills and prepares you to deliver better results in real SAP implementations.
  • Certified professionals are more likely to be chosen for challenging or high-impact projects.
  • Certification gives you recognition within the broader SAP ecosystem.

Exam Topics and Focus Areas

To pass the certification you’ll need a score of 68% or higher, SAP outlines the following key areas:

  • Understanding the Intelligent Enterprise and SAP’s reference architecture
  • Familiarity with SAP BTP services, account models, capabilities (including AI), suite qualities, extensibility types, clean core, and migration strategies (greenfield, brownfield)
  • SAP Methodologies: Integration Solution Advisory Methodology (ISA-M), Application Extension Methodology, and Data & Analytics Methodology
  • Identity and access management types, CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model), RAP (ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model), and runtime environments
  • DevOps principles, SAP BTP commercial models, and master data replication
  • Recommending solution designs based on requirements and compliance

Key Areas of Study

To gain the knowledge required, focus on the following areas:

  • Enterprise Architect vs. Solution Architect roles
  • SAP BTP Framework and Reference Architectures
  • SAP Discovery Centre, Business Accelerator Hub, and Trust Centre
  • SAP BTP account model (global accounts, directories, subaccounts)
  • BTP Identity and Access Management (IAM)
  • BTP Connectivity and SAP Cloud Connector
  • S/4HANA implementation types: Greenfield, Brownfield, Bluefield
  • Application development frameworks and extensibility
  • Clean Core principles and extensibility models
  • SAP BTP Data and Analytics (SAP Datasphere, SAP Analytics Cloud)
  • SAP Business Data Cloud
  • SAP Data and Analytics advisory Methodology
  • SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology (ISA-M)
  • SAP Integration solution Capabilities and Services
  • SAP Build Process Automation, and Signavio
  • SAP AI tools like Joule, and Generative AI Hub
  • SAP BTP Commercial Models and BTP estimating costs

Deep Dive: ISA-M in Practice

ISA-M is central to BTP solution architecture and used throughout the integration lifecycle:

  • Phase 1: Assess your integration strategy (e.g., cloud-to-cloud, on-premise-to-cloud)
  • Phase 2: Design the hybrid integration platform by mapping use case patterns to technology
  • Phase 3: Define best practices, blueprints, and guidelines
  • Phase 4: Establish organizational and operational models for enterprise integration

If you study the Learning journey (FREE) courses listed below, you'll gain the core knowledge needed for this exam.
For deeper insight into areas you may not work with daily, make sure to check out the advanced learning links shared below as well.
The exam time is sufficient , you will not face any challenges there , and by practicing with the BTP trial environment or your organization’s non-production BTP landscape, you’ll be well prepared to pass the exam surely.

Recommended (Free) Learning Courses

  • Becoming an SAP BTP Solution Architect (main course)
  • Discovering SAP Business Technology Platform
  • Delta Content for BTP Solution Architect

Important Reference Links

  • Integration Architecture Guide (ISA-M)
    NoteBookLM Audio Dialog
  • Integration Solution Advisory Methodology PDF Guide
    NoteBookLM Audio Dialog
  • SAP BTP Guideline Framework
  • SAP Application Extension Methodology
  • ISA-M Presentation (PDF)
  • Extension Architecture Guide
  • SAP Data & Analytics Advisory Methodology Overview

Advanced Learning and Practice

  • Administering SAP BTP (Learning)
  • Create a BTP Trial Account (Tutorial)
  • SAP BTP Admin Guide
  • SAP Trust Centre (Security and Compliance)
  • Connecting BTP and On-Pre using the Cloud Connector (Learning)
  • BTP Account Model & Entitlements
  • OpenSAP : ISA-M Integration Assessment (Video)
  • Integration Suite Assessment Tool (Video)
  • Migration Assessment App (Tutorial)
  • Setting up SAP Mobile Start with SAP S/4HANA on SAP Build Work Zone (Learning)
  • Clean Core Three-Tier Extensibility(Learning)
  • SAP Discovery Centre:  Reference Architectures & Estimators
  • Cost Estimator
  • Cloud Integration Automation Service User Guide

I've put together 15 sample questions to help you prepare for the exam. These questions are a bit more challenging than the ones you'll typically see on the actual test.

1. Which statement is true about SAP trail account in BTP?
a. A BTP trial account includes only 6 GB of memory for applications
b. You are able to move your Proof of Concept from BTP trail to Pay-As-You-Go account
c. A trial account will be removed when a user not logged on after 90 days
d. You need to manually restart of your solution like HANA DB every day

2. Which SAP licensing model In SAP BTP supports: "The total monthly cost would be deducted from the cloud credits balance monthly"?
a. SAP BTPEA
b. Pay-As-You-Go for SAP BTP
c. Subscription
d. SAP Full User Equivalent (FUE)

3. In which level of BTP account you can add or modify your Identity Authentication Service was assigned?
a. Global account
b. Subaccount
c. Directory
d. All answers above

4. In SAP Road Map, SAP decided to have the traditional Early Watch Report in which solutions or platform?
a. SAP Solution Manager
b. SAP Cloud ALM
c. SAP Early Watch alert Workspace
d. DVM in SAP For Me launchpad

5. According to the SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology, the Enterprise Architect is responsible for which of the following activities?
a. Definition of integration standards and technology selection guidelines (cross-vendor, hybrid integration platform)
b. Definition of technical integration architectures with a focus on specific integration technologies
c. Definition and communication of patterns, templates, and best practices
d. All of above
Reference: 
Link   page 51

6. A customer has BOBJ and BODS in their existing SAP on premises landscape, when they are going to migrate to S/4 HANA private edition which solution they can pick to support a similar functionality? Make sure the solution required to be aligned with SAP latest future road map.
a. HANA Cloud, Datasphere
b. SAP Analytic Cloud (SAC), SAP S/4 HANA
c. SAP Intelligent Suite, SAP BW cloud
d. SAP Analytic Cloud (SAC), Datasphere

7. Which statement about SAP Clean Core concept is NOT correct?
a. Deploying a new custom code cannot be on core system (by stack).
b. The clean core strategy advocates for separating custom extensions from the core SAP application.
c. Use "Key User Extensibility" functions of S/4HANA for simple extensions.
d. In classic Extensibility: Applicable primarily to on-premises editions, this tier includes traditional customization methods.

8. Your company has S/4 HANA provisioned in National Security Services (NS2) in Azure, for the entry point of external users you as Solution Architect recommending BTP work Zone as an option.
Which answer meets the requirements (as a cost-effective option) for number 1, 2 descriptions in the diagram below:

a. SAP Build Work Zone advanced edition, SAP Connectivity Service
b. SAP Build Work Zone standard edition, SAP Connectivity Service
c. SAP Build Work Zone advanced edition, Cloud Connector
d. SAP Build Work Zone standard edition, Cloud Connector

9. You are acting as a Solution Architect guiding a migration team that is transitioning from an on-premises SAP PI/PO system to the SAP BTP Integration Suite.
Which of the following tools or services should you recommend to the team, in their appropriate sequence, to support this migration effectively?
a.
SAP BTP Migration Assessment
SAP BTP Integration Assessment
SAP Readiness Check
b.
SAP Readiness Check
SAP BTP Migration Assessment
SAP BTP Integration Assessment
c.
SAP Readiness Check
SAP BTP Integration Assessment
SAP BTP Migration Assessment
d.
SAP BTP Integration Assessment
SAP Readiness Check
SAP BTP Migration Assessment

10. Which of the following SAP BTP services are required or typically used by SAP Cloud ALM?
Select all correct answers (3):
a. SAP Alert Notification Service
b. SAP Automation Pilot
c. SAP Cloud Transport Management
d. SAP Build Work Zone
e. SAP Analytic Cloud
f. SAP Conversational AI

11. Which service can provide a rough rate cost for any service you are going to purchase for BTP solution:
a. SAP For Me Legal & cost
b. SAP Cloud estimator
c. SAP BTP Service Catalog
d. SAP BTP Service Estimator

12. A customer is planning to implement Change Request Management (ChaRM) in the cloud after migrating to SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition on a hyperscaler (e.g., Azure).
Which two main tools or technologies can they use to manage change and transport processes in this cloud-based environment?
a. SAP Solution Manager, CTS
b. Cloud Transport Management System (cTMS), SAP Cloud ALM
c. SAP BTP Cloud Foundry, SAP Cloud ALM
d. SAP Solution Manager, Cloud Transport Management System (cTMS)

13. During the Explore phase of an SAP S/4HANA implementation using SAP Activate methodology, a customer aims to validate fit-to-standard processes and ensure alignment with future-state operations.
They also want to establish a monitoring and task management structure early to prepare for efficient project execution and operations.
Which of the following combinations of tools and capabilities are best aligned to support these objectives? (Choose two.)
a. SAP Signavio Process Insights – to compare legacy KPIs against SAP standard benchmarks and identify transformation opportunities
b. SAP Cloud ALM – to import and manage business process models, simulate variants, and execute fit-to-standard workshops
c. SAP Signavio Process Manager – to model target processes and support fit-to-standard workshops using process content from SAP Best Practices
d. SAP Cloud ALM – to orchestrate project tasks, manage requirements, and support test case management from the Explore phase onward
e. SAP Solution Manager – to configure business roles and monitor production incidents during the Explore phase

14. In the context of SAP Event Mesh and SAP BTP, which of the following are required to establish secure communication between publishers and subscribers? (Choose two)
a. XSUAA service for OAuth authentication
b. Connectivity service with destination type = HTTP
c. Application router for handling protocol mapping
d. Event Mesh instance bound to each application with service keys
e. Transport Layer Security (TLS) tunnelling via Cloud Connector

15. What are benefits of using the Cloud Foundry environment in SAP BTP for event-driven extensions? (Choose two)
a. Native ABAP integration without additional runtimes
b. Horizontal scalability for stateless microservices
c. Event handling with built-in webhook configuration
d. Support for event consumers written in various languages (e.g., Node.js, Java)
e. Built-in support for SAP GUI transactions

Answer Keys:
1-Answer: d; 2.Answer: a; 3.Answer: b; 4.Answer: d; 5.Answer: a; 6.Answer: d; 7.Answer: a;
8.Answer: d;9.Answer: b; 10.Answer: a, b, c; 11.Answer: d; 12.Answer: b; 13.Answer: c, d; 14.Answer: a, d;
15.Answer: b, d

To purchase exam attempts if you don’t have SAP Learning Hub license, you can check the link below:            
https://training.sap.com/content/purchase-certification-attempts

Schedule exam for BTP Solution Architect(P_BTPA_2408)

Share with others and Connect with us!

Please leave your comment if you have anything to add!
If you would like to ask questions, please use the community Q&A.
Give us a like and share on social media if you feel it was useful!
You can follow me in People SAP @Amin_Omidy 
Thanks!

Figure 1: Alt Text: Image of Arin Bhowmick, Chief Design Officer, SAP, with the title and ID of his talk, along with date and location for Orlando.

Listen to Arin Bhowmick, our Chief Design Officer:

Title: SAP's user-experience strategy to achieve better outcomes

Orlando: PUX2821, May 20, 2025 3:30 – 3:50 PM EDT

Madrid: PUX1497, May 27, 2025, 1:30 – 1:50 PM CEST

entry.title: 🔄 Mapping Pricing Conditions from SAP CPS to SAP CPQ and Back to S/4HANA Sales Documents
entry.content:
This blog explores how to map pricing conditions from CPS to CPQ, and how to pass them back to S/4HANA Sales Order, Solution Quotation, or Solution Order—ensuring consistency, accuracy, and automation.

Note : Ensure you have Document Pricing is enabled in your SAP CPQ tenant

Understand the difference between Stalesless Pricing vs Document Pricing from the ground level.

In the evolving SAP ecosystem, integrating pricing logic across systems like SAP CPS (Configuration & Pricing Services), SAP CPQ (Configure Price Quote), and S/4HANA is critical for delivering a seamless quote-to-cash process. 

How to set Pricing Procedures to populate the Prices coming from CPS to CPQ and CPQ to backend S/4 HANA

How to map CPS Pricing Condition Types to the SAP CPQ Quote Line Items Fields

🧩 Why Map Pricing Conditions to SAP CPQ Line Items?

SAP CPQ acts as the central quoting engine in many enterprise sales processes. However, pricing logic often resides in Variant Pricing (VP) and Pricing Procedures defined in S/4HANA or CPS. Mapping these conditions to CPQ line items is essential because:

  • Consistency: Ensures pricing in CPQ matches backend systems.
  • Automation: Reduces manual intervention and errors.
  • Transparency: Sales reps see the same pricing logic as backend users.
  • Integration: Enables seamless handover of quotes to S/4HANA for order processing.

 

🔧 Key Components Involved

  1. CPS (Configuration & Pricing Services),– Hosts the pricing logic and variant configuration and pricing.
  2. SAP CPQ – Front-end for sales reps to configure and quote with Document Pricing logic via CPS.
  3. S/4HANA – Backend system for order processing and fulfillment.
  4. Pricing Procedure – Defines how pricing is calculated using condition types.
  5. Condition Types – Represent discounts, surcharges, taxes, etc.
  6. Variant Configuration (VC) – Drives product configuration and stateless pricing logic.
  7. Standard Integrations - SAP provides standard integration packages for placing a order to S/4 HANA Public, Private Cloud and Onprem

 

🛠️ Steps to Map Pricing Conditions from CPS to CPQ

1. Define Pricing Conditions in S/4HANA

  • Create or reuse condition types (e.g., PR00, K004, RA01).
  • Assign them to a pricing procedure.
  • Ensure access sequences are defined for condition records.

2. Expose Pricing Logic via CPS

  • CPS acts as a middleware to expose S/4HANA pricing logic.
  • Use APIs or integration flows to expose condition types and pricing results.

3. Configure SAP CPQ to Consume CPS Pricing

  • Enable SAP Variant Configuration & Pricing (CPS) integration in CPQ.
  • Ensure you have Document Pricing enabled in your CPQ for interaction (interactive Sales Pricing)
  • Map CPS pricing outputs to CPQ line item fields via Setup - Pricing - Pricing Procedures.
  • For custom business requirement, Use scripting or custom logic to parse CPS response and populate CPQ QICF.

4. Map Condition Types to CPQ Line Items

  • Use Standard Quote Item fields or Create Quote Item custom fields in CPQ to hold condition values (e.g., base price, discounts, customer price, Rebates, Surcharges, Free Item.... etc).
  • Use mapping logic to assign CPS condition types to these fields.
  • Ensure the mapping supports multiple condition types per line item.

5. Pass Pricing Back to S/4HANA via Standard Integration Packages

  • When quote is accepted by customer, CPQ sends the configuration and pricing data back via Integration Iflow.
  • Iflow is mapped with all Pricing conditions set in CPQ ( Pricing Procedure Mapping)
  • Iflow will use standard APIs to create:
    • Sales Orders
    • Solution Quotation
    • Solution Order
  • Ensure pricing conditions are passed as part of the payload or re-determined in S/4HANA using the same pricing procedure.

Below is the Integration mapped from SAP CPQ Quote Item fields to SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud - Solution Order

Below is the Integration mapped from SAP CPQ Quote Item fields to SAP S/4 HANA Private Cloud - Solution Quotation

🔄 Round-Trip Pricing Flow

✅ Best Practices

  • Use consistent condition types across systems.
  • Test pricing logic in CPS thoroughly before exposing to CPQ.
  • Use version control for pricing procedures and VC models.
  • Log and monitor CPS calls for performance and accuracy.
  • Train sales teams on how pricing is derived in CPQ.

📌 Conclusion

Mapping pricing conditions from CPS to CPQ and back to S/4HANA is a foundational step in achieving a unified and automated sales process. By aligning pricing logic across systems, businesses can ensure accuracy, reduce errors, and accelerate the quote-to-cash cycle.

entry.title: Revolutionizing Order Management with AI: SAP’s CAS AI/ML Powered Order Management Service
entry.content:
In today’s fast-paced business environment, efficiency and customer satisfaction are paramount. With the availability of SAP Cloud Application Services (CAS) AI/ML Powered Order Management Service, a comprehensive managed service designed to transform how businesses handle sales orders, streamline operations, and elevate customer experiences.

The AI Disruption in Order Management

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) are no longer just buzzwords—they are driving a seismic shift in how businesses operate. The CAS AI/ML Powered Order Management Service is at the forefront of this disruption, embedding advanced AI and ML capabilities to deliver unprecedented levels of efficiency and effectiveness in the order management process.

What is the CAS AI/ML Powered Order Management Service?

This end-to-end, AI-driven service automates and optimizes the entire sales order process, leveraging the power of SAP Business AI, Machine Learning (ML), and Large Language Models (LLMs). Here’s how it works:

  • Automated Order Extraction: The service intelligently parses emails and extracts attachments (PDFs, TIFFs, JPEGs, etc.) to create new orders automatically.

  • Real-Time Master Data Validation: It validates customer and product data in real-time against the S/4HANA system, ensuring accuracy and compliance.

  • AI-Powered Email Responses: Using natural language processing (NLP), the service generates real-time, tailored email responses (in multiple languages) to customers, enhancing communication and satisfaction 

  • Dynamic Order Modifications: Businesses can make real-time updates to orders, adapting to changing customer needs with ease.

  • 24/7 Operational Efficiency: The service operates round-the-clock, eliminating delays and ensuring seamless order processing even during peak demand periods.

How AI and ML Drive Efficiency and Effectiveness

The integration of AI and ML into the order management process brings a new level of precision and speed:

  • Intelligent Automation: By automating repetitive tasks such as order entry, data validation, and email responses, the service reduces manual effort and minimizes errors.

  • Real-Time Decision Making: AI-powered business rules ensure that decisions are made instantly, based on predefined criteria, reducing processing time and improving accuracy.
  • Scalability: The service can handle large volume orders effortlessly, making it ideal for businesses experiencing seasonal surges or rapid growth.

Why Does This Matter?

The CAS AI/ML Powered Order Management Service addresses critical challenges faced by businesses today:

  • High Operational Costs: Reducing the need for manual intervention and staffing during peak seasons.

  • Delayed Customer Responses: Ensuring real-time communication to maintain customer trust and loyalty.

  • Inefficient Sales Processes: Automating repetitive tasks to free up sales teams for value-added activities.

  • Global Reach: Supporting over 40 languages, making it ideal for multinational corporations.

Key Benefits for Businesses

  • Scalability: Handle large volumes of orders effortlessly, even during seasonal surges.

  • Cost Efficiency: Significantly reduce operational costs associated with order processing.

  • Enhanced Customer Satisfaction: Faster order processing and real-time updates lead to happier customers.

  • Error Reduction: Real-time data validation minimizes errors and improves accuracy.

A Managed Service with Immediate Impact

Unlike standalone AI services that require manual setup and configuration, the CAS AI/ML Powered Order Management Service is a fully managed solution. This means businesses can adopt it immediately without the need for complex implementation. It integrates seamlessly with SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP, offering a fixed-scope service that delivers value from day one.

Driving AI Adoption Across Industries

This service not only enhances operational efficiency but also encourages the adoption of SAP’s AI Business Services. By leveraging SAP’s Generative AI Hub, businesses can unlock new opportunities for innovation and growth.

Join the Future of Order Management

The CAS AI/ML Powered Order Management Service is more than just a tool—it’s a game-changer for businesses looking to stay ahead in a competitive market. Whether you’re a multinational corporation or a growing enterprise, this service is designed to meet your needs and exceed your expectations.

SAP Business AI , SAP S/4HANA,

entry.title: Import Sales Documents - Part 2: How to upload numbers with different separators
entry.content:
Dears,

I am glad to introduce you how to upload numbers with different decimal/thousands separators in “Import Sales Documents” apps.

 

Decimal/Thousands Separator Setting in Excel

Basically, the decimal and thousands separator in excel are using the system separators by default (Path: File/Options/Advanced/Use system separators).

If you want to display the numbers with different separator, you can also uncheck the ‘Use system separators’ option and define your own. For example, you set decimal separator to comma, thousands separator to dot. You will see the number as following in excel (2.38 -> 2,38, 3,456.45 -> 3.456,45).

Notes: Please make sure that the cell format is type of number. The default format is Text.

 

Limitations in Different Cell Format

There are some limitations due to the design of this app.

Number Value with Number Format

No matter what decimal separator you are using in the excel and in the launch pad user setting, you will preview the number value in the app with dot decimal separator only.

But please be confident that the value will be correctly assigned to the sales document.

Number Value with Text Format

In case you are using the default Text format, currently, we only support dot decimal separator without any thousands separator. For example, if you have a condition value 3,456.45, you must place it in excel cell as 3456.45. Otherwise, you probability will receive ‘Invalid Format’ error after uploading.

Related Information

The Link Collection for Import Sales Documents | SAP Blogs

Import Sales Documents - SAP Help Portal

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.3: Final What's New & Product Assistance available
entry.content:
With immediate effect, the final What's New information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.3 is now available.

To access the final What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer.

In addition, the final Product Assistance (aka Application Help) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.3 is available. Please use the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Help Portal page to access it.

For you as a customer, we recommend to also use the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.3 will be available in RASD within the next few days. A valid Customer S-user ID is required for login. We recommend to use RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you as a customer.

You can use the commenting feature for What’s New. With this feature, authors can evaluate your feedback and incorporate it into the documentation, if needed. We hope that by opening this channel of communication, we can learn how to further improve our What’s New content for you. To use this feature, you need to be logged into the Help Portal. The feature will only be available in the English version of the documents. To learn more about how the feedback process works in SAP Help Portal, see Giving Documentation Feedback.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud – Network Performance Guidelines
entry.content:
Understanding Network Performance Requirements for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Latency Requirements for Front-End User Access

Latency plays a significant role in the user experience. For SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the recommended latency for front-end user access is 200ms or better. This ensures that users experience minimal delays when interacting with the system, leading to a smoother and more responsive interface.

Bandwidth Requirements for Integration and Access

Bandwidth is another critical factor that affects the performance of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Here are the key bandwidth requirements based on the number of users:

  • Small, trial: 1 Mbps
  • Medium, 50 users: 2 Mbps
  • Large, 100 users: 4 Mbps
  • Extra large, 250 users: 10 Mbps
  • XXL, 500 users: 20 Mbps minimum

Additionally, the minimum upstream and downstream bandwidth requirements are both 1 Mbps.

Accessing the System across Geo Locations

Accessing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition from a different geo-region than the backend system datacenter location is generally not an issue anymore. The E2E response time depends on the latency between both locations, but also on the Internet Service Provider quality. Additionally, the E2E response time also depends on the amount of Fiori application roundtrips (some do have the need to sequential calls).

Here is an external example of a global latency matrix.

 

Connectivity and Integration

The service is designed to support cross-region integration, ensuring that services from various locations can integrate with the S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. For example, if a customer in Australia is consuming a BTP service deployed in AWS Sydney and later decides to deploy SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud service for its subsidiary in EMEA, the integration into the IAS service in AWS Sydney can be managed seamlessly.

It must be considered that the maintenance windows per service follow geo-region time-windows. Distributing a service across geo-regions has the disadvantage of unsynchronized maintenance windows.

 

For more detailed information on network requirements and performance guidelines, you can refer to the following SAP notes:

  • 2240690- Front-end Network Bandwidth Sizing for SAP Fiori Apps and SAP S/4HANA
  • 2916959- Fiori Performance Troubleshooting
  • 1100926- FAQ: Network performance

By adhering to these guidelines, you can ensure that your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition deployment runs smoothly, providing excellent user experience and efficient integration.

 

Ensuring Optimal Performance

To ensure optimal performance, it's essential for SAP Cloud Delivery teams to monitor and adjust the system resources dynamically. The customer system is constantly monitored, and system quota adjustments happen accordingly. This proactive approach helps in maintaining the required performance levels without any customer interaction for application server adjustments.

If the customer sees the need to adjust the database size, he can create a support ticket for the delivery team to upsize the database (on additional costs) within the next maintenance window downtime.

 

Q/A Section:

  1. Q: Sizing and capacity management: How do we address the seasonality in a customer’s business, for example retail around Black Friday in the US at Thanksgiving.

A: S/4HANA Public Cloud is being offered as a SaaS solution with rich extension capabilities. Our operations team ensures monitoring and a proper performance.

At the same time customers influence the performance by utilizing customer extensions and APIs as well as how the end user is using the system.

The production system will be sized based on the number of acquired FUEs/the different packages. In addition, available resources will be adapted dynamically. In case a project wants to verify the sizing, they can execute a quick sizer project. Depending on the result, it would be recommended to contact SAP.

Further information:

Benchmark | Sizing | SAP -> Tab Quick Sizer -> HANA-based Cloud Quick Sizer

The customer system is constantly monitored and certain checks/thresholds indicate whether a system need to be enhanced. System quota adjustment happen.

No customer interaction is required for application server adjustments.

Customer can raise a service request ticket, if he can foresee the need for enhancement too (e.g. because of peak-season). They can raise a ticket for database enhancement too (additional costs)

2. Q: Because customers share resources in the Public Cloud, how do we ensure one customer does not take all the resources from the other customers?

A: On Database level, resources are assigned per customer and constantly being monitored and can be adjusted to the growth required by customer. On Application Server level, dedicated application servers are assigned per customer. They are as well being monitored and can be adjusted according to the needs and growth.

3. Q: What are the customer frontend system and browser requirements / technical prerequisites? Which network bandwidth is needed?

A: Some SAP notes on frontend and network pre-requisites:

2240690 - Front-end Network Bandwidth Sizing for SAP Fiori Apps and SAP S/4HANA - SAP ONE Support Launchpad

2916959 - Fiori Performance Troubleshooting - SAP ONE Support Launchpad -> see recommendations in the Network section

Front-End Network Requirements for SAP Business Solutions -> the guide includes a formular to calculate network requirements (chapter 4)

1100926 - FAQ: Network performance - SAP ONE Support Launchpad

 

Helpful info from ByD: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/acb47640133745ee96b255b1b975da28.html?version=2105

Network Requirements by number of users

Small, trial: 1 mbps

Medium, 50 users: 2 mbps

Large, 100 users: 4 mbps

Extra large, 250 users: 10 mbps

XXL, 500 users: 20 mbps minimum

 

Minimum requirements

Upstream: 1 mbps

Downstream: 1 mbps

Latency: 200ms or better

4. Q: Performance and load testing: Are these tests performed and if so, how often? - Are they performed for the entire platform or are they also performed on a per module basis? - When was the last set of tests conducted? - What were the results?

A: Performance tests are performed as per the product standard, e.g. before a new version of the application is released.

Performance tests are conducted on a modular level.

Performance tests results for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition are not publicly available. 

For On Premise customers there is an example available in sizing guide: https://help.sap.com/doc/a2d4dcb162b94a08b33599b49ee0c6d5/6.0/en-US/EHSM_6_Sizing_Guide.pdf

entry.title: 无启用采购订单工作流审批功能，却报审批工作流错误
entry.content:
大家好：

相信会有很多客户启用了采购订单工作流审批的功能，但也会有部分客户没有启用这个功能，如果有客户没有启用该功能，但是发现创建的采购订单报错如下：

这时首先需要检查APP：管理工作流-采购订单中是否有激活的工作流，如果没有并且确定不需要启用该功能请检查APP:输出参数设定 中 采购订单---渠道

检查分派时间是否有值，如果有请删除后再创建采购订单。这样上述错误就会消失，采购订单会正常创建。

具体原因请见SAP Note:https://me.sap.com/notes/3572781/E

entry.title: Become a Certified SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Project Manager
entry.content:
In the dynamic world of digital transformation, where businesses are constantly evolving to stay competitive, project management is more crucial than ever. Central to this landscape is the role of professionals equipped to manage complex cloud projects. With the newly launched SAP Certification for Managing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Projects (C_S4PM), you can now become a certified project manager and take charge of leading transformative initiatives.

Designed for Project Managers, Consultants, and Technical Leads, this certification empowers you to excel in managing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementations, positioning you as a key player in your organization's digital transformation projects with strategic guidance and expert integration.

How can you prepare for this certification?

You can prepare for the exam in your own pace, by completing the "Managing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Projects" learning journey, designed to provide comprehensive insight into SAP S/4HANA Cloud project management. With free access to the learning journey, you can deepen your expertise of managing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementations, specifically focusing on SAP tools, methodologies, and SAP Business Suite portfolio.

Additionally, SAP Community blog posts provide some supporting information to deepen your understanding and preparation:

  • "Guiding Your Cloud Journey: The New SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Adoption Plan Roadmap"
  • "SAP Partner: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Implementation Tools Overview"
  • "News from SAP Activate - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 and more"

You can also explore more insights about SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition on SAP for Me and taking a look at the release notes.

As a prerequisite, candidates should possess project management experience, preferred in ERP implementation, especially SAP Cloud-related projects. Additionally, hands-on experience with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition solutions and experience with managing cloud-based SAP projects is beneficial.

Kickstart your journey today

By becoming a certified project manager for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you will be able to position yourself as a key contributor to your organization's growth and innovation. You can demonstrate a high level of competency in orchestrating SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition projects, showcasing expertise and leadership in cloud initiatives that are aligned with business objectives.

Certified learners need to continuously keep their skills up-to-date and certifications valid by completing mandatory assessments before their certification expires. With an SAP Learning Hub subscription, you can prepare for and take the stay certified assessments at any time during the validity period of your certification.  If you want to know more on how to get and stay certified, you can read this blog post.

Open new professional opportunities for you with new learning and a sought-after certification. Build the skills you need on the SAP Learning site and get inspired by reading learner stories on SAP Community, ask questions and connect with fellow learners. When you’re ready, take your certification exam and start shaping your industry's future!
